scope: branch changes vs origin/dev (issue #1131 — DOCKER_GID detection fix)

non_findings_reviewed.note: Probe runs `docker run --rm -v /var/run/docker.sock:... alpine stat ...` with an unpinned `alpine` tag and the docker socket mounted. Operator-run deploy script, not attacker-reachable; the operator already trusts the daemon and the images `compose up` pulls. Excluded (hard-exclusion #5, missing hardening / no exploit path). Optional hardening: pin alpine by digest.

non_findings_reviewed.surface: DOCKER_GID=0 outcome on Docker Desktop / Colima / rootless
non_findings_reviewed.note: Probe now correctly resolves GID 0 (root group) on Docker-Desktop-class runtimes, so the backend joins supplementary group 0 to reach a root:root-owned socket. Not an added escalation: docker-socket access is already root-equivalent by design (Invariant #11), and reaching a root-group-owned socket as non-root REQUIRES group-0 membership — there is no lower-privilege alternative. Native Linux prod is unchanged (probe returns the non-zero host docker GID). Excluded.
`sed -i.bak "s/^DOCKER_GID=$/DOCKER_GID=${detected}/"` — `${detected}` is numeric-only on both source paths (`tr -dc '0-9'` probe; numeric GID field via `cut -d: -f3`). No injection. Verified.

Swallow site changed from print() to throttled logger.warning(..., e). The exception is a Docker socket/permission error (PermissionError / docker.errors.APIError) — no credentials or env in the message. Net security improvement (silent swallow -> Vector-observable). Excluded.

Triggered on `pull_request` (NOT pull_request_target) with a top-level `permissions:` block; runs a hermetic shimmed bash test, no secrets, no `${{ github.event.* }}` in run steps. `actions/checkout@v6` is first-party/unpinned (MEDIUM per FP rule) and consistent with the entire existing workflow — not introduced by this diff. Excluded.

verdict: PASS — no reportable security findings at the 8/10 daily gate. Infra correctness + observability fix; no new attack surface, secrets, injection, auth, or disclosure.
